claim: REQ-010 pins pty:exit as the LAST pane-scoped frame, but the session's onData callback forwards ANY backend data as a pty:data evt without a liveness check - with the real node-pty backend a data event delivered after onExit (post-exit flush straggler) would emit pty:data AFTER pty:exit, breaking the ordering invariant the client will key teardown on; the fake backend cannot exhibit this (dead-guard), so the frozen suite cannot catch it.
fix: Guard the onData callback with panes.has(id) (true during pre-exit flush, false only after paneExited) so a post-exit straggler is dropped instead of violating the exit-last contract.
resolution: Fixed in src/agent/session.ts onData wiring: the callback now early-returns unless panes.has(id) (in-code comment cites FINDING-001), so a post-exit backend straggler can never emit pty:data after pty:exit while the legitimate pre-exit flush window (panes.has true until onExit->paneExited) is preserved. Verified: read the guarded code; full agent suite (56) + whole-repo suite green after the edit; implement gate re-derived green on the final tree; codex read-only cross-check independently reported the SAME defect against the pre-fix tree (its only finding), confirming both the defect and that the fix addresses the exact path it named.

claim: REQ-013's 'a live pane MUST NOT keep the process alive' is proven only through the fake backend: the real node-pty backend's teardown sends the default signal (SIGHUP) and the agent then waits for natural loop drain - a shell/child that ignores SIGHUP keeps its pty fds open and can hold the remote agent process alive after stdin end, exactly the orphan class the epic's supervision open-question owns; no v1 CI vector can exercise this (Linux-only real backend), so it is a disclosed real-backend residual, not a frozen-suite gap.
fix: Defer to the F18/F19 hardening (session survival + provisioning own agent-process lifetime): add a bounded SIGKILL escalation after kill() in the real backend, verified on a real Linux host; no F16 action beyond recording.
